# consolidate-facts — LLM Reference

Merges and deduplicates facts.json files that have grown past the 40-fact threshold. Keeps memory lean so session-start context stays fast.

## Triggers — use /consolidate-facts when

- Health check warns about facts.json over threshold (>40 entries)
- /oms-audit flags memory bloat
- Facts file is producing noisy or redundant session-start output

## Invocation

```
/consolidate-facts             # consolidate all bloated projects
```

## What it does

1. Finds all facts.json files with >40 entries across ~/.claude/projects/
2. Runs mem0.py consolidate on each — deduplicates, merges related facts
3. Verifies result (target: ≤25 facts per project)
4. Re-runs health-check.sh to confirm no remaining warnings

## Key files

Read: `~/.claude/projects/*/facts.json`
Write: same facts.json files (overwritten on success only — unchanged on API failure)
Uses: `~/.claude/hooks/memory-persistence/mem0.py consolidate`
Model: Haiku (~$0.001 per run)

## What it doesn't do

- Never touches MEMORY.md or topic files — only facts.json
- Never deletes facts — consolidates and deduplicates only
- Never overwrites on API failure — safe to run anytime
